steps.description: Fmoc-rink amide AM resin (0.78 mmol/g) was weighed out and the beads were allowed to swell in DMF for 1 hour.
Fmoc deprotection was achieved with 20% v/v piperidine in DMF, added to the resin and left to agitate for 15 min.
After Fmoc deprotection, the resin was thoroughly washed with DMF.

2 equiv. of HOBt and HBTU were dissolved in DMF and combined. 2 equiv. of the initial protected amino acid pre-dissolved in DMF were introduced, followed by 2 equiv. of DIPEA, allowing 20 seconds for activation. The solution was added to the resin and left to agitate for 45 minutes. Coupling was monitored via Kaiser test.
The resin was washed with DMF and subjected to a series of deprotection and coupling reactions with the desired amino acids (His, His, Lys, Val, Ile, Leu). All reactions were performed at 25°C.
After thorough washing with DMF, a DMF solution of 12 equiv. of Ac2O and DIPEA was added (capping) to prevent side reactions of the side-groups, and the mixture was incubated for 30 minutes.
For P1, the Fmoc group of the final amino acid was deprotected. Then, the N-terminus of P1 was acetylated using a 5-times excess of Ac2O and DIPEA.
The resin was subsequently washed with DMF and DCM and allowed to dry before the peptide cleavage step.
Peptide cleavage was performed using a mixture of 94% TFA, 3% Milli-Q water and 3% TIS.
The solvents were reduced under an argon atmosphere.
Et2O was added to precipitate the peptide.
The peptides were isolated through several centrifugations, washed with Et2O, and dried under reduced pressure.
The peptides were lyophilized in Milli-Q water with the presence of 0.01% TFA and analyzed by HPLC-MS. Purity ranged between 95 and 99%.

synthesis.notes: P1 (Ac-LIVKHH-NH2) was synthesized using Fmoc/Boc solid-phase peptide synthesis. The N-terminus was acetylated with Ac2O/DIPEA after Fmoc deprotection of the final amino acid. Yield: 287 mg (57.4%). ESI-MS: Calculated for C37H62N12O7 (786.98, [M+H+]+), found m/z 787.52. Purity 95-99% by HPLC-MS, no further purification required.

steps.description: Weigh out Fmoc-rink amide AM resin (0.78 mmol/g) and allow beads to swell in DMF for 1 hour.
Fmoc deprotection: add 20% v/v piperidine in DMF to the resin and agitate for 15 min, then wash thoroughly with DMF.

Dissolve 2 equiv. of HOBt and HBTU in DMF and combine. Add 2 equiv. of the initial protected amino acid pre-dissolved in DMF, then add 2 equiv. of DIPEA and allow 20 seconds for activation. Add this solution to the resin and agitate for 45 minutes. Wash resin with DMF. Monitor coupling via Kaiser test.
Repeat deprotection and coupling reactions with each desired amino acid (L, I, V, K, H, H) in sequence. All reactions performed at 25°C, couplings monitored via Kaiser test.
After thorough washing with DMF, add a DMF solution of 12 equiv. of Ac2O and DIPEA (capping) to prevent side reactions of the side-groups. Incubate for 30 minutes.
For P2, the N-terminus is left intact with the Fmoc group still attached (no acetylation step). Wash resin with DMF and DCM and allow to dry.
Cleave peptide from resin using a mixture of 94% TFA, 3% Milli-Q water, and 3% TIS.
Reduce solvents under an argon atmosphere.
Add diethyl ether (Et2O) to precipitate the peptide.
Isolate peptide through several centrifugations, wash with Et2O, and dry under reduced pressure.
Lyophilize peptide in Milli-Q water with 0.01% TFA.

steps.description: Fmoc-rink amide AM resin was weighed out and allowed to swell in DMF for 1h.
Fmoc deprotection using 20% v/v piperidine in DMF with agitation.
Coupling of amino acids using HOBt, HBTU, and DIPEA in DMF. The process is repeated for the sequence LIVKHH.
Capping of side groups with Ac2O and DIPEA.
Deprotection of the final amino acid's Fmoc group followed by N-terminus acetylation.
Peptide cleavage from resin using a TFA/Water/TIS mixture, followed by precipitation in diethyl ether.
Lyophilization in Milli-Q water with 0.01% TFA.

steps.description: Fmoc-rink amide AM resin is weighed and allowed to swell in DMF for 1h.
Fmoc deprotection using 20% v/v piperidine in DMF with agitation.
Coupling of amino acids: 2 equiv. of HOBt and HBTU in DMF are combined with 2 equiv. of protected amino acid and 2 equiv. of DIPEA (activated for 20s).
Capping of side groups using 12 equiv. of Ac2O and DIPEA in DMF.
Peptide cleavage from resin using a mixture of TFA, Milli-Q water, and TIS (94:3:3).
Solvents reduced under argon and Et2O added to precipitate the peptide.
Peptides isolated by centrifugation, washed with Et2O, dried under reduced pressure, and lyophilized in Milli-Q water with 0.01% TFA.

starting_materials.name: Fmoc protected amino acids (leucine, isoleucine, valine, lysine, histidine)

steps.description: Fmoc-rink amide AM resin was weighed out and beads were allowed to swell in DMF
Fmoc deprotection with 20% v/v piperidine in DMF, added to resin and agitated
Washing with DMF, then 2 equiv. of HOBt and HBTU dissolved in DMF and combined
2 equiv. of protected amino acid pre-dissolved in DMF introduced, followed by 2 equiv. of DIPEA, allowing 20 seconds for activation
Solution added to resin and left to agitate for coupling reaction
Resin washed with DMF and subjected to series of deprotection and coupling reactions with desired amino acids, monitored via Kaiser test
After washing with DMF, capping with 12 equiv. of Ac2O and DIPEA in DMF to prevent side reactions
For P1, Fmoc group of final amino acid deprotected, then N-terminus acetylated using 5-times excess of Ac2O and DIPEA
Resin washed with DMF and DCM and allowed to dry before peptide cleavage
Peptide cleavage using mixture of 94% TFA, 3% Milli-Q water and 3% TIS
Solvents reduced under argon atmosphere, and Et2O added to precipitate the peptide
Peptides isolated through several centrifugations, washed with Et2O, and dried under reduced pressure
Peptides lyophilized in Milli-Q water with 0.01% TFA

equipment.name: HPLC-MS
equipment.settings: Ultimate 3000 HPLC system coupled with maXis UHR-TOF

synthesis.notes: The peptide P1 (Ac-LIVKHH-NH2) was synthesized using Fmoc/Boc solid-phase peptide synthesis. Yield: 287 mg (57.4%). Purity ranged between 95-99%, no further purification necessary. ESI-MS: Calculated for C37H62N12O7 (786.98), found m/z 787.52 ([M+H+]+).

starting_materials.name: Fmoc protected amino acids (leucine, isoleucine, valine, lysine, histidine)

steps.description: Fmoc-rink amide AM resin was weighed out and beads were allowed to swell in DMF
Fmoc deprotection with 20% v/v piperidine in DMF added to resin and agitated
Resin washed with DMF, then 2 equiv. of HOBt and HBTU dissolved in DMF and combined
2 equiv. of protected amino acid pre-dissolved in DMF introduced, followed by 2 equiv. of DIPEA, allowing 20 seconds for activation
Solution added to resin and left to agitate for coupling reaction
Resin washed with DMF and subjected to series of deprotection and coupling reactions with desired amino acids (Leu, Ile, Val, Lys, His)
After washing with DMF, capping with 12 equiv. of Ac2O and DIPEA in DMF to prevent side reactions
For P2, N-terminus remained intact with Fmoc group still attached (no acetylation)
Resin washed with DMF and DCM, then dried before peptide cleavage with 94% TFA, 3% Milli-Q water, 3% TIS
Solvents reduced under argon atmosphere, Et2O added to precipitate the peptide
Peptides isolated through several centrifugations, washed with Et2O, and dried under reduced pressure
Peptides lyophilized in Milli-Q water with 0.01% TFA

equipment.name: HPLC-MS
equipment.settings: Ultimate 3000 HPLC system coupled with maXis UHR-TOF

equipment.name: ESI-MS
equipment.settings: maXis UHR-TOF equipped with ESI electrospray ionization chamber in positive mode
